commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.sourceCode:
import React from 'react';
import { Box, Card, Divider, Heading, Stack, Text } from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface Props {
  /** City name */
  city: string;
  /** Current temperature */
  temperature: number;
  /** Weather condition (Sunny, Cloudy, Rainy, etc.) */
  condition: string;
  /** Humidity percentage */
  humidity: number;
  /** Wind speed */
  windSpeed: number;
  /** Temperature unit (C or F) */
  unit?: string;
  /** 5-day forecast array */
  forecast: Array<{ day?: string; icon?: string; high?: number; low?: number }>;
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({
  city,
  temperature,
  condition,
  humidity,
  windSpeed,
  unit = 'C',
  forecast,
}: Props) {
  const conditionIconFallback =
    condition && condition.trim().length > 0 ? condition.trim().charAt(0) : '•';

  return (
    <Card
      shadow="md"
      radius="lg"
      border
      padding="var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)"
    >
      <Stack
        gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
      >
        <Heading
          level={4}
          color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
        >
          {city}
        </Heading>

        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          justify="between"
          align="center"
          wrap
          gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
        >
          <Text
            as="div"
            size="4xl"
            weight="bold"
            color="var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)"
          >
            {temperature}°{unit}
          </Text>

          <Stack align="center" gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
            <Box
              padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
              background="var(--ggui-color-surface, #fafafa)"
              borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
            >
              <Text
                as="span"
                size="2xl"
                color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
              >
                {conditionIconFallback}
              </Text>
            </Box>
            <Text
              variant="bodyLarge"
              weight="medium"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
              align="center"
            >
              {condition}
            </Text>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>

        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          justify="between"
          wrap
          gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
        >
          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
            <Text
              variant="caption"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            >
              Humidity
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="label"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
            >
              {humidity}%
            </Text>
          </Stack>

          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
            <Text
              variant="caption"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            >
              Wind Speed
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="label"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
            >
              {windSpeed}
            </Text>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>

        <Divider color="var(--ggui-color-outlineVariant, #e4e4e7)" margin={0} />

        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          justify="between"
          wrap
          gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
        >
          <Text
            as="span"
            variant="caption"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
          >
            5-day forecast
          </Text>
        </Stack>

        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          justify="between"
          wrap
          gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
        >
          {forecast.map((item, index) => {
            const dayLabel = item.day ?? '--';
            const iconLabel = item.icon ?? conditionIconFallback;
            const highLabel = item.high !== undefined ? `${item.high}°` : '--';
            const lowLabel = item.low !== undefined ? `${item.low}°` : '--';

            return (
              <Box
                key={item.day ?? index}
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
                background="var(--ggui-color-surface, #fafafa)"
                borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
                aria-label={`${dayLabel}: ${highLabel} / ${lowLabel}`}
              >
                <Stack
                  align="center"
                  gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)"
                >
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    weight="medium"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                  >
                    {dayLabel}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    as="span"
                    size="lg"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)"
                  >
                    {iconLabel}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                  >
                    {highLabel} / {lowLabel}
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Box>
            );
          })}
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

evaluation.critique: The component correctly renders all weather data from props with proper fallbacks and formatting. Design tokens are consistently applied throughout, though some hardcoded fallback color values undermine token usage. The layout is generally responsive but lacks visual polish with missing hover states, transitions, and interactive feedback on forecast items.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

OpenAI SDK iterative: no compiled code produced

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.sourceCode:
import React from 'react';
import { Card, Stack, Text } from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface Props {
  /** City name */
  city: string;
  /** Current temperature */
  temperature: number;
  /** Weather condition (Sunny, Cloudy, Rainy, etc.) */
  condition: string;
  /** Humidity percentage */
  humidity: number;
  /** Wind speed */
  windSpeed: number;
  /** Temperature unit (C or F) */
  unit?: string;
  /** 5-day forecast array */
  forecast: Array<{ day?: string; icon?: string; high?: number; low?: number }>;
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({
  city = 'City',
  temperature = 0,
  condition = 'Unknown',
  humidity = 0,
  windSpeed = 0,
  unit = 'C',
  forecast = [],
}: Props) {
  return (
    <Card
      padding="var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)"
      shadow="md"
      radius="lg"
      border
    >
      <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)">
        <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
          <Text
            variant="overline"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            as="p"
          >
            Weather
          </Text>
          <Text
            variant="bodyLarge"
            weight="semibold"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
            as="p"
            aria-label={`Current weather in ${city}`}
          >
            {city}
          </Text>
        </Stack>

        <Stack direction="horizontal" justify="between" align="center" gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)">
          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
            <Text
              size="4xl"
              weight="bold"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
              as="p"
              aria-label={`Current temperature ${temperature}°${unit}`}
            >
              {temperature}°{unit}
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
              as="p"
            >
              {condition}
            </Text>
          </Stack>

          <Text
            size="3xl"
            as="span"
            aria-label={`Condition icon ${condition}`}
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
          >
            {condition}
          </Text>
        </Stack>

        <Stack direction="horizontal" justify="between" gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)">
          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)" align="start">
            <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)">
              Humidity
            </Text>
            <Text variant="label" color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)">
              {humidity}%
            </Text>
          </Stack>

          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)" align="start">
            <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)">
              Wind
            </Text>
            <Text variant="label" color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)">
              {windSpeed}
            </Text>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>

        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          wrap
          gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
          justify="between"
          aria-label="5 day forecast"
        >
          {forecast.map((item, index) => (
            <Card
              key={`${item.day ?? 'day'}-${index}`}
              padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
              shadow="none"
              radius="md"
              border
            >
              <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)" align="center">
                <Text
                  variant="caption"
                  weight="medium"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                  as="p"
                >
                  {item.day ?? '—'}
                </Text>
                <Text
                  size="xl"
                  as="span"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                  aria-label={`${item.day ?? 'Day'} weather icon`}
                >
                  {item.icon ?? '•'}
                </Text>
                <Text
                  variant="caption"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                  as="p"
                >
                  {item.high ?? '--'}° / {item.low ?? '--'}°
                </Text>
              </Stack>
            </Card>
          ))}
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

evaluation.critique: The component properly uses design tokens for colors and spacing with good semantic variable choices (onSurface, onSurfaceVariant). However, the layout has issues: the forecast strip uses wrap which may cause poor responsive behavior on mobile, and the main temperature section displays the condition text twice (once as label, once as a large icon placeholder). The weather condition icon is rendered as text rather than actual emoji, reducing visual polish.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

The message presentation and hierarchy are strong, though interactive polish could benefit from transition effects on messages and hover states on message bubbles.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

Minor issues: some hardcoded pixel values in `Spinner size` props and `padding={80}` bypass spacing tokens; a few fallback hex colors in token declarations are unnecessary since tokens should be primary. commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

Several issues: hardcoded color value 'var(--ggui-color-error-500, #ef4444)' with fallback hex, inconsistent use of design tokens (padding uses raw numbers like 32, 24, 20, 12, 8 instead of var(--ggui-spacing-*)), and missing avatar validation error display. commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card

commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

The layout is incomplete (code cuts off mid-component), making it impossible to assess grid implementation, search functionality, legend, and detail panel. Missing interactive states and proper spacing variable usage significantly impact polish and layout quality.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

While data presentation is strong with proper timestamp formatting and sender grouping, there are several polish gaps including missing hover states on the send button, inconsistent spacing usage (hardcoded 16/12/8px values), and the message bubble shadows use inline styles instead of token references.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.
